title: Two Sum
description: Given an array of integers and a target, return the indices of the two numbers that add up to the target. Exactly one solution exists; you may not reuse an element.
constraints: 2 <= nums.length <= 1e4; -1e9 <= nums[i] <= 1e9; exactly one valid answer.

title: Merge Sorted Array
description: You are given two sorted integer arrays. The first has length m+n, with only the first m entries meaningful and the rest zero-filled padding; the second has length n and is fully populated. Merge the second array into the first in place so the first array becomes one sorted array of length m+n, then return it.
constraints: nums1.length == m+n; nums2.length == n; 0 <= m, n <= 200; -1e9 <= nums1[i], nums2[j] <= 1e9.

title: Remove Element
description: Given an array of integers and a value, remove every occurrence of that value in place, keeping the relative order of the remaining elements irrelevant, and return the count of elements that are not equal to the value.

title: Majority Element
description: Given an array of integers, return the value that appears in more than half of the elements. It is guaranteed such an element exists.
constraints: 1 <= nums.length <= 5e4; -1e9 <= nums[i] <= 1e9; a majority element always exists.

title: Rotate Array
description: Given an integer array, rotate it to the right by k steps (k can exceed the array length) and return the rotated array.

title: Best Time to Buy and Sell Stock
description: Given daily stock prices, choose one day to buy and a later day to sell to maximize profit, and return that maximum profit (0 if no profit is possible).
constraints: 1 <= prices.length <= 1e5; 0 <= prices[i] <= 1e4.

title: Best Time to Buy and Sell Stock II
description: Given daily stock prices, you may buy and sell as many times as you like (holding at most one share at a time). Return the maximum total profit achievable.
constraints: 1 <= prices.length <= 3e4; 0 <= prices[i] <= 1e4.

title: Jump Game
description: Each element of an array is the maximum jump length from that position. Starting at index 0, determine whether the last index is reachable.
constraints: 1 <= nums.length <= 1e4; 0 <= nums[i] <= 1e5.

title: Jump Game II
description: Each element of an array is the maximum jump length from that position, and the last index is always reachable. Return the minimum number of jumps needed to reach the last index starting from index 0.
constraints: 1 <= nums.length <= 1e4; 0 <= nums[i] <= 1000; last index always reachable.

title: H-Index
description: Given the citation counts for a researcher's papers, compute the h-index: the largest value h such that the researcher has at least h papers with at least h citations each.
constraints: 1 <= citations.length <= 5000; 0 <= citations[i] <= 1000.

title: Insert Delete GetRandom O(1)
description: Design a data structure supporting average O(1) insert(val), remove(val), and getRandom() (uniformly random among current elements). Given a sequence of operations and their arguments, return the sequence of results (insert/remove return whether the set changed; getRandom returns an element currently present).
constraints: -2^31 <= val <= 2^31-1; at most 2e5 calls total; getRandom is called only when the structure is non-empty.

title: Product of Array Except Self
description: Given an integer array, return an array where each element is the product of all the other elements (not itself), without using division and in O(n) time.
constraints: 2 <= nums.length <= 1e5; -30 <= nums[i] <= 30; product of any prefix/suffix fits in a 32-bit signed integer.

title: Gas Station
description: There are gas stations in a circle, each with a gas amount and a cost to travel to the next station. Starting with an empty tank, find the index of the station to start from to complete the full circuit, or return -1 if impossible. A solution, if it exists, is unique.
constraints: gas.length == cost.length; 1 <= gas.length <= 1e5; 0 <= gas[i], cost[i] <= 1e4.

title: Candy
description: Children stand in a line, each with a rating. Every child gets at least one candy, and a child with a strictly higher rating than either neighbor must get more candy than that neighbor. Return the minimum total candies needed.
constraints: 1 <= ratings.length <= 2e4; 0 <= ratings[i] <= 2e4.

title: Trapping Rain Water
description: Given an elevation map represented by non-negative bar heights of width 1, compute how much rainwater it can trap after raining.
constraints: 1 <= height.length <= 2e4; 0 <= height[i] <= 1e5.

title: Roman to Integer
description: Convert a Roman numeral string (using symbols I, V, X, L, C, D, M with standard subtractive notation) to its integer value.
constraints: 1 <= s.length <= 15; s is a valid Roman numeral in the range [1, 3999].

title: Integer to Roman
description: Convert an integer in the range [1, 3999] into its Roman numeral representation using standard subtractive notation.

title: Length of Last Word
description: Given a string of words separated by spaces, return the length of the last word (a word is a maximal run of non-space characters).
constraints: 1 <= s.length <= 1e4; s consists of English letters and spaces; s contains at least one non-space character.

title: Longest Common Prefix
description: Given an array of strings, find the longest prefix common to all of them. Return an empty string if none exists.
constraints: 1 <= strs.length <= 200; 0 <= strs[i].length <= 200; strs[i] consists of lowercase English letters.

title: Reverse Words in a String
description: Given a string, reverse the order of the words (maximal runs of non-space characters), collapsing any leading, trailing, or multiple in-between spaces to a single space between words.
constraints: 1 <= s.length <= 1e4; s contains English letters, spaces, and digits; s has at least one non-space character.

title: Zigzag Conversion
description: Write a string in a zigzag pattern across a given number of rows (top to bottom, then diagonally back up, repeating), then read the characters row by row and return the resulting string.
constraints: 1 <= s.length <= 1000; 1 <= numRows <= 1000; s consists of English letters, commas, and periods.

title: Find the Index of the First Occurrence in a String
description: Given a haystack string and a needle string, return the index of the first occurrence of the needle in the haystack, or -1 if the needle does not occur.
constraints: 1 <= haystack.length, needle.length <= 1e4; both consist of lowercase English letters.

title: Text Justification
description: Given a list of words and a line width, greedily pack words onto lines, then space-pad each line so left and right edges are flush (extra spaces distributed as evenly as possible, leftmost gaps get any remainder). The last line, and any line with a single word, is left-justified with trailing spaces to fill the width.
constraints: 1 <= words.length <= 300; 1 <= words[i].length <= 20; 1 <= maxWidth <= 100; words[i] consists of English letters and no spaces.

title: Valid Palindrome
description: Given a string, determine whether it reads the same forwards and backwards after lowercasing and removing every character that is not a letter or digit.

examples.input: s = "A man, a plan, a canal: Panama"

constraints: 1 <= s.length <= 2e5; s consists of printable ASCII characters.

input: A man, a plan, a canal: Panama

title: Is Subsequence
description: Given strings s and t, determine whether s can be formed by deleting some (possibly zero) characters from t without reordering the remaining characters.
constraints: 0 <= s.length <= 100; 0 <= t.length <= 1e4; both consist of lowercase English letters.

title: Two Sum II - Input Array Is Sorted
description: Given a 1-indexed array sorted in non-decreasing order, find two numbers that add up to a target and return their 1-indexed positions as [index1, index2] with index1 < index2. Exactly one solution exists.
constraints: 2 <= numbers.length <= 3e4; -1000 <= numbers[i] <= 1000; numbers is sorted non-decreasing; exactly one solution exists.

title: Container With Most Water
description: Given heights of vertical lines at each index, choose two lines that together with the x-axis form a container holding the most water, and return that maximum area.
constraints: 2 <= height.length <= 1e5; 0 <= height[i] <= 1e4.

title: 3Sum
description: Given an integer array, return all unique triplets [a,b,c] (as a list of index-independent triplets, not indices) with a+b+c == 0. The solution set must not contain duplicate triplets.
constraints: 3 <= nums.length <= 3000; -1e5 <= nums[i] <= 1e5.

title: Minimum Size Subarray Sum
description: Given an array of positive integers and a target sum, find the length of the shortest contiguous subarray whose sum is at least the target. Return 0 if no such subarray exists.
constraints: 1 <= target <= 1e9; 1 <= nums.length <= 1e5; 1 <= nums[i] <= 1e4.

title: Longest Substring Without Repeating Characters
description: Given a string, find the length of the longest contiguous substring that contains no repeated characters.
constraints: 0 <= s.length <= 5e4; s consists of English letters, digits, symbols, and spaces.

title: Substring with Concatenation of All Words
description: Given a string s and an array of equal-length words, find every starting index in s where a substring is exactly a concatenation, in any order, of each word in the array exactly once (with no extra characters in between).
constraints: 1 <= s.length <= 1e4; 1 <= words.length <= 5000; 1 <= words[i].length <= 30; all words have the same length; s and words[i] consist of lowercase English letters.

title: Minimum Window Substring
description: Given strings s and t, find the shortest contiguous substring of s that contains every character of t (including duplicates) at least as many times as it appears in t. Return an empty string if no such window exists.
constraints: 1 <= s.length, t.length <= 1e5; s and t consist of English letters.

title: Valid Sudoku
description: Given a 9x9 board where 0 marks an empty cell, determine whether the currently filled cells satisfy Sudoku rules: no repeated digit 1-9 in any row, column, or 3x3 box (empty cells are ignored and the board need not be solvable/complete).

examples.input: a partially filled 9x9 board with no row/column/box conflicts

constraints: board.length == board[i].length == 9; board[i][j] is 0 (empty) or 1-9.

title: Spiral Matrix
description: Given an m x n matrix, return all its elements visited in spiral order starting from the top-left, going right, then down, then left, then up, spiraling inward.

title: Rotate Image
description: Given an n x n matrix representing an image, rotate it 90 degrees clockwise in place and return it.
constraints: n == matrix.length == matrix[i].length; 1 <= n <= 20; -1000 <= matrix[i][j] <= 1000.

title: Set Matrix Zeroes
description: Given an m x n matrix, if an element is 0, set its entire row and column to 0, in place, and return the modified matrix.

title: Game of Life
description: Given a board of 0 (dead) and 1 (live) cells, compute the next generation under Conway's rules (a live cell with 2-3 live neighbors survives, a dead cell with exactly 3 live neighbors becomes alive, all others die/stay dead) simultaneously for every cell, and return the updated board.
constraints: m == board.length; n == board[i].length; 1 <= m, n <= 25; board[i][j] is 0 or 1.
